question.text: Higher interest rates in the economy affect real estate and auto sectors more intensely because:

answer.text: These sectors have zero debt levels
Most buying in these sectors by middle-class happens through loans which become expensive
These sectors are not affected by interest rates at all
Higher rates increase demand for these sectors

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Higher rates affect real estate and auto more because most buying by middle-class people happens through loans (home loans, car loans), which become expensive with higher rates, reducing demand. **The Trap:** These are interest-rate sensitive sectors. Higher rates reduce, not increase, demand. **Cross-Exam Context:** Rate sensitivity of sectors tested in NISM Series XV and IV. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When RBI signals rate cuts, real estate and auto stocks often rally in anticipation of improved demand.
